info.title: ESD Ticket Booking Platform API
info.description:
Microservices-based event ticket booking platform for IS213.

All responses follow a standard envelope:
- **Success**: `{"code": N, "data": {...}}`
- **Error**: `{"code": N, "message": "..."}`

All endpoints accept an optional `X-Correlation-ID` header (UUID). If not provided, one is auto-generated for distributed tracing.

tags.name: Seats
tags.description: Seat and section management with Redis+MySQL dual locking

tags.name: Notifications
tags.description: Notification history and OTP verification (SMU Lab Notification API)

tags.name: Fees
tags.description: Service fee and refund tracking (Charging service)

tags.name: Orchestrator
tags.description: Booking Orchestrator â€” saga-based composite service

get.description:
Retrieve all events, optionally filtered by status or category.
Note: OutSystems returns PascalCase fields (Id, Name, EventDate, etc.) â€” the frontend normalizes to snake_case.

post.description:
Creates an event directly in OutSystems. Does NOT create seat sections â€” use
`POST /api/orchestrator/events/create` for the full flow (event + seats).

description.example: An explosive night of funk, soul, and pop.

200.description: Event created (OutSystems does not return the new event ID)

put.description:
Partial update of an event in OutSystems. Only provided fields are changed.
Used by the Booking Orchestrator to sync AvailableSeats.

AvailableSeats.description: PascalCase â€” used by orchestrator availability sync

post.description:
Cancels an event directly in OutSystems. Does NOT publish to RabbitMQ â€”
use `POST /api/orchestrator/events/{id}/cancel` for the full cancel flow
that triggers downstream refunds and notifications.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an event from the OutSystems database.

post.description:
Bulk-updates all events with empty Status fields to "upcoming".
Utility endpoint to fix data quality issues in OutSystems.

get.description: Retrieve bookings, optionally filtered by event or status.
Retrieve all bookings for a user, sorted newest first.

post.summary: Create sections and seats for an event

409.description: Sections already exist for this event

get.summary: Get all seats for an event
get.description: Returns all seats with their section information.
Returns available seat count per section for the given event.

404.description: No sections found for this event

post.description:
Reserve a seat using Redis+MySQL dual locking. If the requested seat is
taken but another seat in the same section is available, the service may
auto-assign a different seat (indicated by `auto_assigned: true` and the
`originally_requested` field in the response). If the entire section is
full, returns 409 with an `other_sections` array of sections that still
have availability.

other_sections.description: Sections with remaining availability (returned when entire section is full)

post.description: Release a seat reservation. Returns 403 if the caller is not the lock owner.
Transitions a reserved seat to booked status and removes the Redis lock.
Creates a Stripe PaymentIntent in SGD currency.
Verifies a Stripe PaymentIntent status and returns transaction details.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of notifications to return

post.description: Sends a one-time password to the given mobile number via Twilio Verify.

get.description: Returns the ticket for a booking, including a base64-encoded QR code image.

post.description: Marks a ticket as invalidated (used during refunds).

Validates a scanned QR code at event entry. Verifies the HMAC hash,
checks ticket status, and marks it as "used". Returns 409 if already
used, 410 if invalidated (refunded/cancelled), 403 if hash mismatch.

QR data format: `{booking_id}:{sha256_hash_first_16_chars}`

Used by the admin QR scanner page (`/scanner`). The frontend enriches
the response with event name and seat details from the Event and Seat services.

qr_data.description: Raw QR code data string (format booking_id:hash)

200.description: Ticket validated and marked as used

post.description: Adds the user to the waitlist for a sold-out event. Returns 409 if already on waitlist.

409.description: User already on waitlist for this event

get.description: Returns the user's current position on the waitlist, along with promotion details if applicable.

404.description: User not on waitlist for this event

get.summary: Get all fees for an event
get.description: Returns all fee records for the event along with a summary (count, total fees, total refunds).

post.description:
Starts the booking saga: validates the event, reserves a seat, creates a
booking record, and creates a Stripe PaymentIntent. Returns details needed
for the frontend to complete payment.

409.description: Event cancelled, not bookable, or seat taken

post.description:
Finalizes the booking saga after successful payment. Verifies payment,
confirms the seat, updates booking status, and triggers ticket generation
and notifications via AMQP.

409.description: Saga not in correct status for confirmation

post.description:
Processes a voluntary refund for a confirmed booking. Refunds via Stripe,
invalidates the ticket, releases the seat, and applies a service fee.
Refunds are blocked within 24 hours of the event.

403.description: User is not the booking owner

409.description: Booking not in refundable status or within 24h cutoff

get.description: Returns the full SagaLog object for monitoring saga progress.

post.description:
Creates an event in OutSystems and sets up seat sections atomically.
If seat setup fails, the event creation is rolled back.

Cancels an event in OutSystems and publishes a cancellation message to
RabbitMQ to trigger downstream refund and notification workflows.

409.description: Event is not in a cancellable state

CorrelationID.name: X-Correlation-ID
CorrelationID.description: Correlation ID for distributed tracing. Auto-generated if not provided.

status.description: Defaults to pending if not provided

auto_assigned.description: True if a different seat was assigned because the requested one was taken

originally_requested.description: The originally requested seat_id (present only when auto_assigned is true)

verification_sid.description: Twilio Verify SID for subsequent verification

qr_code_data.description: Raw data encoded in the QR code

qr_code_base64.description: Base64-encoded PNG image of the QR code

promoted_seat_id.description: Seat ID assigned upon promotion (null if not yet promoted)

promotion_expires_at.description: Deadline to accept promoted seat (null if not yet promoted)

client_secret.description: Stripe client secret for frontend payment confirmation

total_seats.description: Total number of seats created across all sections

steps.description: Ordered list of saga steps and their outcomes
